steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence describes the Wheeling National Heritage Area. Although the opening phrase mentions recognition "in 2000," the blank describes what the area itself IS — a defining, ongoing characteristic, not a completed past event.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'had been': past perfect implies the state has ended and preceded another past event. Nothing in the sentence supports that the area stopped being a region with those attributes. ✗
  • Choice B — 'was': simple past implies the state no longer holds. The passage gives no indication the area's character changed after 2000. ✗
  • Choice C — 'is': simple present correctly describes a defining, ongoing state. The area still exists and still holds those natural, cultural, and historical attributes today. ✓
  • Choice D — 'will have been': future perfect projects completion at a future point. The sentence has no future time reference to anchor this tense. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
